hook: Some days I want it so bad it scares me.
slide2: I've been thinking about this thing I want. Like, obsessively. And then the moment it feels close, I find a reason it won't work. Classic me. Sabotage as a personality trait.
slide3: The scary part isn't failing. It's getting it and then having nothing left to want. What do I do with myself after? I've been chasing this so long I forgot who I am without the chase.
slide4: I keep saying I'm 'not ready yet.' But honestly? I think I'm just terrified of finding out what I'm actually made of. Ready is a lie I tell myself so I never have to find out.
closer: So... are we scared of failing or scared of succeeding?
caption:
The funniest joke is wanting something with your whole chest and then lowkey praying it doesn't actually happen.

Like what is that. Who gave us this brain.

I'm not self-sabotaging, I'm just... doing extensive research on worst case scenarios.

Anyone else want something so much it physically makes you want to run away from it?
